title: Clippy: 6 format_push_string — use write!/push_str
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Performance & Complexity
**Lint:** clippy::format_push_string

## Violation
`s.push_str(&format!(...))` allocates a throwaway String just to copy its bytes into `s`. `write!(s, ...)` avoids the intermediate allocation.

## Locations
- src/prompt.rs: 163, 165, 182
- src/tui/app.rs: 763, 885, 897

## Mandated Refactor
```rust
use std::fmt::Write;
// Before
s.push_str(&format!("foo {x}\n"));
// After
write!(s, "foo {x}\n").unwrap();  // write! on String cannot fail
```
For plain literals without interpolation use `.push_str("literal")`.
